query_strategy_description: 22 web-search queries grouped into 6 buckets that map to the report's 15 sections. Use WebSearch (native Claude tool). Avoid named-firm aggregator results; prefer listed-company filings, exchange disclosures, competition-authority records, trade-press deal coverage, and primary research firms with cited methodology. The competitive_landscape blueprint is more filing-and-press heavy than market_analysis — most of the lift is identifying the right 10 names and triangulating their share + recent moves.

query_buckets.purpose: Industry shape, HHI, fragmentation index, segment split -> drives 07_industry_structure + 05_exec_summary_snapshot
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} HHI Herfindahl index OR market concentration ratio

query_buckets.purpose: Identify top 10 named players and pull revenue / share / ownership -> drives 08_competitor_matrix + 09_share_evolution + 11/12/13_player_profiles
expected_sources: Tokyo Stock Exchange + KRX filings (for parent groups)
query_buckets.note: Iterative bucket — first 2 queries surface the name set, then queries 3-4 are re-run per named player. Budget 6 queries here but expect to spend 8-10 in practice (replanned per player).

query_buckets.purpose: Strategic group axes, positioning data -> drives 10_strategic_groups_map + 11/12/13_player_profiles
expected_sources: analyst-firm positioning notes (read but not cited by name)
query_buckets.expected_tag_mix: secondary 50% / estimate 40% / primary 10% (KIRA places players on the map)

query_buckets.purpose: Pricing tier data per player -> drives 14_pricing_positioning
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} pricing benchmark per unit OR per SKU {{year}}
expected_sources: company catalogs + price lists on product pages
query_buckets.note: B2B services often have opaque quoted pricing — if 3+ players have no public band data, swap 14_pricing_positioning to a buyer-segment positioning chart and reduce this bucket to 1 query.

query_buckets.purpose: M&A deals + named partnerships in last 24-36 months -> drives 15_ma_timeline + the 'recent moves' field in player profiles
expected_sources: Mergermarket / DealStreetAsia equivalents (cited only via primary deal docs)
regional business press (Nikkei Asia, The Edge, VnExpress Business, KrAsia, etc.)

query_buckets.purpose: New entrants, adjacent threats, foreign players entering -> drives 16_disruptor_watch
queries: {{country}} {{industry}} funding rounds Series A B startups {{year}}
expected_sources: regional VC tracker press (Tech in Asia, e27, KrAsia, DealStreetAsia)
startup funding databases (cited via primary press releases)
incumbent company commentary on new competition
query_buckets.note: Disruptor scoring is a KIRA primary call. Use trade press to identify candidates, then triangulate threat-tier in the bucket-06 synthesis step. Most cards land as [primary] tagged.

query_execution_rules.parallelism: Run queries in parallel within a bucket (up to 5 concurrent). Sequential across buckets — bucket 02 must complete before buckets 03-06 can re-bind {{leading_player_candidate}}.
result_filtering: Discard results from named-firm aggregators (Mordor, Frost, Euromonitor, IMARC, Synovate, Ipsos, etc.) — anti-positioning rule
Prefer results with citeable filing IDs (exchange tickers, regulator docket numbers, press release URLs)
If a player's revenue appears in 2+ independent sources within +/-15%, treat as well-anchored secondary
If only one source carries the number, either find corroboration or downgrade to KIRA estimate with explicit anchor commentary
For private players with no filings, build the revenue estimate from observable proxies (store count x avg revenue, employee count x revenue-per-FTE, share x total market size) and tag [estimate]
query_execution_rules.estimate_methodology_template: Anchored estimate: {{anchor_metric}} x {{ratio}} = {{output}}. E.g. 'Total market USD {{X}}bn x player share {{Y}}% from regulator filing -> player revenue USD {{Z}}m'.
query_execution_rules.research_data_output_format: Aggregate findings into a single JSON file matching the shape of references/research_data_R0152.json — top-level keys for: industry_structure, top_10_players (array of objects with revenue / share / parent / segment / recent_moves / pricing_band / positioning_xy), share_evolution, ma_events, disruptors. Each value tagged with source URL + access date + extraction method.

template_variable_resolution.{{year}}: Topic year (parser output, default current year)
template_variable_resolution.{{leading_player_candidate}}: Filled iteratively as bucket 02 runs — first two queries surface the name set; queries 3-6 re-bind per player. Player profile sections (11/12/13) pull from the resolved rank-1/2/3 names.
template_variable_resolution.{{concentration_descriptor}}: Derived after bucket 01: 'highly concentrated' (HHI > 2500), 'moderately concentrated' (1500-2500), 'unconcentrated' (<1500), 'fragmenting' (HHI down >300 over 5y), 'consolidating' (HHI up >300 over 5y)
template_variable_resolution.{{x_axis}}: Chosen by analyst from positioning data, e.g. 'price tier', 'distribution breadth', 'digital maturity'
template_variable_resolution.{{y_axis}}: Second strategic axis, paired with x_axis for the 2x2 — must produce real spread, not a diagonal
